item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is absolutely underrated.
I loved it when I translated it into Italian.
I hope the community will discover it and love it like I do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait you didn't mean to write about scp-3000 for the void in the mind caused my amnestics? That's interesting…
SCP-3000 is infiltrating the minds once more

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even after claiming that he had finally made the "perfect" piece

instant +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why does the foundation continue experiments when this SCP seems to have been more or less compliant, cooperative, and is warning them to "Please Stop". It seems foolish that the foundation continues the experiments… and then theres the bit referring to SCP-3000…. it certainly makes 3000 seem alot more sinister then it already was…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Apart from the unnatural sounding dialogue, everything about this anomaly is too convenient. It was human-created, but it has effectively omnipotent wish-granting power, already knows about the Foundation, can interact with dreams (and thereby knows about amnestics / 3000) and manifests helpful explanatory notes, even though the same information could have been provided via interview. My suspension of disbelief was obliterated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is referring to amnestics in D-Class minds, but I'm kicking myself now for not thinking about 3000 when writing this. Could've had some very interesting implications.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the statue actually referring to amnestics within the minds of D-Class, which may or may not have been produced by SCP-3000?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't really want to touch this too much now that it's up but I decided to fix up the first point a little, hope that makes it a little better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a tough one. On one hand, it's a very cool SCP; some kind of statue creature that can mess with people's dreams and grant wishes. Overall structure and tone are also quite good. With that said, there's some weirdness here.

  • The test long could use work. The details which are focused on are chosen oddly; it glosses over how three normal people were apparently teleported next to a bed but dig deep into financial transactions. Also, why does the creature have to leave a note if it can just, you know, talk to researchers?
  • The dialogue in general is kind of janky. Writing good dialogue is hard, especially when the one talking is an SCP; in general, less is more.
  • Linking to a story directly from an SCP the way you did doesn't feel right. Maybe instead have an explicit callout; "SCP-3228 is believed to have been referring to incident such and such" or something.

Overall I really like elements of this but there are a number of issues as well. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Image from: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/e/e9/Godess.jpg

Cheers to the following people for going through the draft:

  • SunnyClockwork
  • Tufto
  • My mate Ben
  • riyangendut
  • Jishwa
  • Anyone else who I've forgotten the name of
